title: Chapter XXVIII.

source.title: ANF06. Fathers of the Third Century: Gregory Thaumaturgus, Dionysius the Great, Julius Africanus, Anatolius, and Minor Writers, Methodius, Arnobius

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Manes said: You are caught in the charge you yourself bring forward.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: For you have been speaking now against yourself, and have not perceived that, in trying to cast reproaches in my teeth, you lay yourself under the greater fault.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Tell me this now, I pray you: if, as you allege, those who have died from the time of Tiberius on to the days of Probus are to say to Jesus, “Do not judge us if we have failed to do Thy works, for Thou didst not send the Paraclete to us, although Thou didst promise to send Him;” will not those much more use such an address who have departed this life from the time of Moses on to the advent of Christ Himself?

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: And will not those with still greater right express themselves in terms like these: “Do not deliver us over to torments, seeing that we had no knowledge of Thee imparted to us?”

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: And will it only be those that have died thus far previously to His advent who may be seen making such a charge with right?

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Will not those also do the same who have passed away from Adam’s time on to Christ’s advent?

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: For none of these either obtained any knowledge of the Paraclete, or received instruction in the doctrine of Jesus.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: But only this latest generation of men, which has run its course from Tiberius onward, as you make it out, is to be saved: for it is Christ Himself that “has re-deemed them from the curse of the law;” as Paul, too, has given these further testimonies, that “the letter killeth, and quickeneth no man,” and that “the law is the ministration of death,” and “the strength of sin.”

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Archelaus said: You err, not knowing the Scriptures, neither the power of God.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: For many have also perished after the period of Christ’s advent on to this present period, and many are still perishing,—those, to wit, who have not chosen to devote themselves to works of righteousness; whereas only those who have received Him, and yet receive Him, “have obtained power to become the sons of God.”

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: For the evangelist has not said all have obtained that power; neither, on the other hand, however, has he put any limit on the time.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: But this is his expression: “As many as received Him.”

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Moreover, from the creation of the world He has ever been with righteous men, and has never ceased to require their blood at the hands of the wicked, from the blood of righteous Abel to the blood of Zacharias.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: And whence, then, did righteous Abel and all those succeeding worthies, who are enrolled among the righteous, derive their righteousness when as yet there was no law of Moses, and when as yet the prophets had not arisen and discharged the functions of prophecy?

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Were they not constituted righteous in virtue of their fulfilling the law, “every one of them showing the work of the law written in their hearts, their conscience also bearing them witness?”

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: For when a man “who has not the law does naturally the things contained in the law, he, not having the law, is a law unto himself.”

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: And consider now the multitude of laws thus existing among the several righteous men who lived a life of uprightness, at one time discovering for themselves the law of God implanted in their hearts, at another learning of it from their parents, and yet again being instructed in it further by the ancients and the elders.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: But inasmuch as only few were able to rise by this medium to the height of righteousness, that is to say, by means of the traditions of parents, when as yet there was no law embodied in writing, God had compassion on the race of man. and was pleased to give through Moses a written law to men, since verily the equity of the natural law failed to be retained in all its perfection in their hearts.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: In consonance, therefore, with man’s first creation, a written legislation was prepared which was given through Moses in behoof of the salvation of very many.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: For if we reckon that man is justified without the works of the law, and if Abraham was counted righteous, how much more shall those obtain righteousness who have fulfilled the law which contains the things that are expedient for men?

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: And seeing that you have made mention only of three several scriptures, in terms of which the apostle has declared that “the law is a ministration of death,” and that “Christ has redeemed us from the curse of the law,” and that “the law is the strength of sin,” you may now advance others of like tenor, and bring forward any passages which may seem to you to be written against the law, to any extent you please.
